personSummary.value: Enjoys flower arranging and responds especially well to soft blue objects, her blue shawl, gentle praise, and short lullabies. Nighttime routines should keep the shawl within reach before lights-out. She may call for her older sister when unsettled at night. A gentle countdown helps evening medication and other stepwise care.

value.description: Lights-out without the blue shawl nearby recently led to calling out and gripping the bed rail.
Blue shawl placement and a gentle countdown supported evening medication acceptance.

citations.quote: used a gentle countdown before each step

value: Place the blue shawl over her shoulders before evening care.
Use a gentle countdown before each medication or transfer step.
Offer blue flowers first during flower arranging and praise the color choice.

citations.quote: placed the blue shawl over her shoulders

value: Lights-out before the blue shawl is within reach.
Bedtime without the familiar lullaby cue.
Rushing nighttime care while she is calling for her sister.

citations.quote: Room lights were turned off before the blue shawl was within reach
called out for her older sister

value.approach: Confirm the blue shawl is within reach, play a short lullaby, then dim the room.

citations.quote: Staff restored the shawl routine and she settled

value.approach: Use the blue shawl and a gentle countdown before each step.

citations.quote: short lullaby was part of their bedtime routine

handoffBrief.value: Yumi Takahashi relies on the blue shawl, soft blue visual cues, gentle praise, and a bedtime lullaby; do not turn lights out before the shawl is in reach.

value.claim: Watch nighttime calling out when the shawl routine is skipped or delayed.

citations.quote: called out and gripped the bed rail
